primary_factors: Primary hazard is a narrow 3–8 AM window of freezing drizzle + freezing fog, creating patchy black ice on bridges/secondary roads and potentially very low visibility near bus time
Negligible snowfall (~0.2 in) and light winds: not a plowable event; Michigan districts usually stay open unless icing is widespread at 6–7 AM
Plow/treatment timing is tight: precip tapering ~6 AM leaves ~0.5 hour before many bus routes—main corridors likely treated, but local/residential pickup roads may lag
No confirmed neighboring district closures or Rockford Public Schools announcement yet; minimal community “closure buzz,” which usually correlates with staying open or a delay
Power outages appear to be improving and not Kent/Rockford-concentrated based on latest available proxy data; unlikely to force a closure by itself
decision_rationale:
Integrating the experts: Meteorology + Web verification agree there is no extreme-cold trigger and snow totals are negligible; the credible disruption pathway is icing and/or dense freezing fog near the morning commute. Safety and Infrastructure both flag that the timing is the problem—freezing drizzle persisting to about 6 AM leaves too little buffer for uniform treatment of secondary/local roads and bridges before buses roll, even if primary routes improve. News intel lacks confirmed closure cascades or official district signals, which (in Michigan) usually keeps full-closure probability low; however, there is some emerging ground-truth of icy conditions and a reported spinout, supporting at least a non-trivial risk that conditions verify worse than “patchy.” Power grid risk appears to be decreasing and is not currently a dominant closure driver.

Brier-score-wise in Michigan, a full closure on a trace-snow event is uncommon unless icing is widespread at 6–7 AM. So the best calibrated call is: low-to-moderate closure odds (15%) but a meaningfully higher chance of a 2-hour delay if fog/ice verify. Operational recommendation: be prepared to choose a DELAY based on 5:30–6:15 AM road checks (especially bridges/rural connectors) and visibility observations.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 1 round(s) with 17.8% spread. Average probability: 10%.
